items.title: Code Review is the Last Unautomated Knowledge Transfer Mechanism
items.next_action: Scout: scan for recent data on engineering time spent in code review across enterprise teams. Need numbers to anchor the thesis.
items.notes: Core product thesis. If this is wrong, Forge doesn't need to exist.

items.title: AI Code Tools Will Bifurcate: Generation vs. Understanding
items.next_action: Synthesizer: build a thesis from the competitive landscape — which tools are generation-focused vs. understanding-focused?
items.notes: Positioning thesis. Forge is in the 'understanding' category. Need to prove this bifurcation is structural, not temporary.

items.title: Series B Investor Narrative
items.next_action: Planner: design the narrative arc for Series B pitch. Start with Stage 1 Excavation.
items.notes: Need compelling narrative that connects product thesis to market opportunity. Board wants to see this by end of Q2.

items.title: Dev Tools Thought Leadership Campaign
items.next_action: Scout: identify top dev tools publications and conferences accepting submissions in the next 3 months.
items.notes: Three articles + one talk. Production order: Substack first, LinkedIn second. Topics should flow from THS-001 and THS-002.

items.title: Competitive Analysis: AI Code Review Landscape
items.next_action: Industry Analyst: map the AI code review competitive landscape. Full Step 0-9 methodology.
items.notes: Need this for both product positioning (Build) and investor narrative (Raise). Feed output to Investment Analyst for market sizing.

items.title: Write 'Why Code Review is Broken' article
items.next_action: Writer: draft article from THS-001 once thesis reaches 'mature' status.
items.notes: First Substack article. Establishes Maya as a voice in the dev tools space.

meta.notes: Seeded by Onboarding Agent. 6 items across 3 goals (Build, Raise, Lead).
